title: dryopsis mariformis
intro: Dryopsis mariformis, also known as the Japanese wood fern, is a species of fern native to Japan and parts of Asia. This plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asia, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Dryopsis mariformis include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and are typically consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
